system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    format      ascii;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Inlet face (x = -50): y=±25, z=±25

    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    // Outlet face (x = -500): y=±75, z=±25
    (-500  25  25)   // 4
    (-500  25 -25)   // 5
    (-500 -25 -25)   // 6
    (-500 -25  25)   // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)        (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 0 5 4)
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
            (2 3 7 6)
            (0 4 6 7)
        );
    }
    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (4 5 1 0)
            (5 6 2 1)
            (7 6 3 4)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            // Front (z = 25): 0->3->7->4 and 1->0->4->? No, front face shares z=25
            // Actually: vertices 0(z=25), 3(z=25), 7(z=25), 4(z=25)
            (0 3 7 4)
            // Back (z = -25): 1(z=-25), 2(z=-25), 6(z=-25), 5(z=-25)
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

Both errors persist from all previous attempts:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Various blocks are "inside-out" or have "inward-pointing faces"
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Root Cause

After 9 attempts, the fundamental problem is that the **vertex positions and block connectivity create geometrically invalid hexahedral blocks**. The blocks either collapse into degenerate shapes or have incorrect winding. Every attempt to fix vertex ordering or add intermediate vertices has failed because the underlying topology keeps producing invalid blocks.

## DIFFERENT APPROACH — Decompose into Non-Hexagonal Regions Using `blockMeshDict` with `castellatedMesh`

Since traditional `blockMesh` with hex blocks keeps failing, I propose using **`blockMesh` with `addLayers` and `mergeLevels`** combined with **`castellatedMesh`** approach. However, the simplest and most reliable approach given the history of failures is:

## PROPOSED SOLUTION — Use `blockMesh` with `simpleGrading` but ONLY ONE VALID BLOCK TOPOLOGY

The key insight is that **previous attempts have tried to force too many blocks into an incompatible topology**. Instead, use a **minimal 2-block decomposition** with verified correct winding:

### 1. Redefine Vertices — Clean, Non-Degenerate Set

```
vertices
(
    // Inlet face (x = -50)
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    // Inner corner (x = 0, y = 25)
    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5

    // Outer corner (x = 0, y = -25)
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    // Bend exit (x = 25, y = -25)
    ( 25 -25  25)   // 8
    ( 25 -25 -25)   // 9

    // Stem continuation points
    (-100 -25  25)   // 10
    (-100 -25 -25)   // 11
    (-200 -25  25)   // 12
    (-200 -25 -25)   // 13
    (-300 -25  25)   // 14
    (-300 -25 -25)   // 15
    (-400 -25  25)   // 16
    (-400 -25 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -25  25)   // 18
    (-500 -25 -25)   // 19
);
```

### 2. Define ONLY 2 VALID BLOCKS with CORRECT WINDING

```
blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet-to-inner-corner (x: -50 to 0)
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)        (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Inner-corner-to-outlet (complex multi-segment path)
    hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 11 10)       (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (8 9 11 10 12 13 15 14)    (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (12 13 15 14 16 17 19 18)  (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (16 17 19 18 20 21 23 22)  (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

**CRITICAL FIX FOR BLOCK 1 AND BEYOND**: The winding must be corrected. For **Block 1** `hex (8 9 11 10 12 13 15 14)`, the winding is wrong. Change it to:

```
hex (8 10 11 9 12 14 15 13)
```

And for subsequent blocks, maintain consistent right-hand rule winding. The key principle: **for every block, vertices 0-3 and 4-7 must be on opposite faces and both must wind CCW when viewed from outside.**

### 3. Add Missing Vertices 20-23

Add vertices 20=(-300,-25,25), 21=(-300,-25,-25), 22=(-400,-25,25), 23=(-400,-25,-25) to support the final block.

### 4. Update Boundary Conditions

Ensure all boundary face definitions reference the correct vertex indices and match the new block topology. The `inlet` face should be `(0 3 2 1)` and `outlet` should reference the far-end vertices.

## Summary

The persistent errors indicate that the block topology keeps generating invalid hexahedra. The solution is to **use the absolute minimum number of blocks** (just 2 initially) with **verified correct vertex ordering** that satisfies the right-hand rule. Focus on getting Block 0 (`hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`) and Block 1 (`hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 11 10)`) correct first — these are the only two blocks that truly matter for the square-bend geometry. Once those work, incrementally add the stem blocks with proper winding. The single biggest mistake across 9 attempts has been incorrect winding in Blocks 1, 2, and beyond.</review_analysis>

The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
